name: Pyrrhotite_hex
comments: Locality is from Ryugu asteroid. Comments: Samples with hexagonal plate structure. The set of Raman bands at 225 cm−1 and 275 cm−1 are consistent with the Fe-S stretching modes of amorphous pyrrhotite, and the band at ~ 313 cm− 1 is consistent with the local tetrahedral lattices of Fe(III) formed on the pyrrhotite surface, denoted as Fe(II)1-3xFe(III) 2xS. Laser 0.6 mW power. From https://doi.org/10.1016/j.gca.2024.06.016

name: b-CaB4O7_
comments: Locality is from synthtetic. Comments: From https://doi.org/10.1016/j.saa.2013.07.072 Oxoborates b-CaB4O7 were synthesized in a high-pressure/high-temperature experiment. A stoichiometric mixture of two parts B2O3 (>99.9%, Strem Chemicals, Kehl, Germany) and one part ZnO (>99%, Merck, Darmstadt, Germany)/CaO (99.95%, Alfa-Aesar, Karlsruhe, Germany) were finely ground together in an agate mortar, filled into a crucible made of hexagonal boron nitride (HeBoSint P100, Henze BNP GmbH, Kempten, Germany), built into an 14/8 (ZnO/B2O3) or 18/11 (CaO/B2O3) assembly and compressed by eight tungsten carbide cubes (TSM10, CERATIZIT Austria GmbH, Reutte, Austria).
